_framework: OMB M-25-22 — Federal Zero Trust Strategy
_description: Maps Cipher-tube controls to OMB M-25-22 zero-trust maturity pillars. Each pillar defines target capabilities, Cipher-tube's implementing components, and current maturity level.

pillars.name: Identity
pillars.target: Agency staff use enterprise-managed identities to access applications. Phishing-resistant MFA protects personnel from sophisticated online attacks.

controls.requirement: Enterprise-managed identity for all users and services
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: All contributors authenticate via GitHub-managed identity with GPG/SSH-signed commits. Automated agents use Ed25519-signed tokens scoped to their pillar with TTL-based expiry.
GitHub organization enforces 2FA. Repository branch protection requires signed commits (GPG/SSH). Automated agents use cryptographic identity tokens — no password-based auth.
controls.notes: GitHub org 2FA enforcement configured at org level, not in repo code.

controls.requirement: Identity lifecycle management with automated provisioning and deprovisioning
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: Trust-tier system manages contributor lifecycle (T0→T4). Agent credentials revocable via kill-switch by T3+. Quarterly drift detection suspends divergent agents. CODEOWNERS auto-assigns based on team membership.

pillars.name: Devices
pillars.target: The agency maintains a complete inventory of every device it operates and can detect and respond to incidents on those devices.

controls.requirement: Complete device inventory and endpoint detection
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: CI runners are ephemeral GitHub-hosted containers — inventoried by workflow definition. Self-hosted runners (if any) must be registered in governance/infrastructure/runners.json.
controls.notes: Device pillar primarily applies to deployment environments. Cipher-tube CI uses ephemeral runners. Production deployment device controls are out of scope for this repository.

pillars.name: Networks
pillars.target: Agencies encrypt all DNS requests and HTTP traffic, and begin segmenting networks around applications.

controls.requirement: Encrypt all traffic (DNS, HTTP) and segment networks
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: All inter-pillar communication encrypted via mTLS with TLS 1.3 enforcement. No TLS 1.2 fallback without explicit Sentinel-reviewed justification. Certificate pinning between pillars. Tube Layer segments network boundaries between Sentinel, Bolt, and Palette.

Pillar-based architecture provides logical segmentation. Cross-pillar calls must traverse the Tube Layer, which validates caller identity and trust tier at every boundary crossing. Dead-letter handling prevents lateral movement via failed messages.

pillars.name: Applications and Workloads
pillars.target: Agencies treat all applications as internet-connected, routinely subject applications to rigorous testing, and welcome external vulnerability reports.

controls.requirement: All applications treated as internet-connected with rigorous testing
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: Every PR undergoes SAST (CodeQL, Semgrep), SCA (dependency audit), secret detection (detect-secrets, TruffleHog), OPA policy evaluation, and pillar-specific testing (perf benchmarks, a11y audits). Minimum 80% line coverage for new code.

controls.requirement: Welcome external vulnerability reports with clear process
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: SECURITY.md defines responsible disclosure via GitHub Security Advisory, encrypted email, and private contact form. Safe harbor policy protects good-faith researchers. Response SLAs published (Critical ≤7d, High ≤14d, Medium ≤30d).

controls.requirement: Immutable workloads and infrastructure as code
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: Infrastructure defined via Docker Compose and Makefiles. CI pipelines are declarative YAML. Governance policies are version-controlled Rego files. Performance baselines committed to repo.

pillars.name: Data
pillars.target: Agencies are on a clear, shared path to deploy protections that make use of thorough data categorization. Agencies take advantage of cloud security services to monitor access to sensitive data, and have implemented enterprise-wide logging and information sharing.

controls.requirement: Data categorization and protection based on sensitivity
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: Safer-Tube checklist enforces PII/PHI masking in logs and telemetry. Data-minimization and retention policies referenced in compliance section. External inputs sanitized into DATACONTEXT — never SYSTEMINSTRUCTION.

Tamper-evident hash-chain audit ledger records all security-relevant actions. Logs are immutable, append-only, and integrity-verified by CI. Structured audit events include actor identity, action, target, timestamp, and outcome.

controls.requirement: Encryption of data at rest and in transit
controls.ciphertubeimplementation: AES-256-GCM for data at rest. TLS 1.3 for data in transit. Key material managed via designated secrets manager. Safer-Tube checklist items enforce both.
